content.title: New US Ebola patient arrives in Germany for treatment
content.summary:
Aid worker flown to Berlin as Trump administration bars Americans from traveling to US on commercial flights

A US national who contracted Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo has arrived in Germany for treatment, the health ministry in Berlin said on Monday, weeks after another American infected with Ebola in the DRC

content.title: Killings continue on Del Monte farm in Kenya, families say, after G4S hired for security
content.summary:
Exclusive: Three men killed in incidents over past year allegedly involving G4S guards, who replaced in-house team after previous deaths

Bereaved families and politicians have raised alarm about continued killings on Del Monte’s pineapple farm in Kenya despite the company hiring a British security firm to replace its in-house security team after previous deaths were exposed by the Guardian.

The multinational food company appointed G4S to guard the farm, which is estimated to cover

content.title: First patients enrolled in record-breaking Ebola treatment trial in DRC
content.summary:
Two drugs are being trialled in the Ituri region in a programme set up just six weeks after the outbreak was declared, with hopes it will reduce mortality rates

There is no approved drug to help the medical teams scrabbling to save lives in the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo – but there are hopes that could change within months as the first patients are enrolled in a treatment trial.

It is a record pace to set up and start this kind of research, scientists

content.title: Nigeria says army has killed 300 bandits in north-western state of Zamfara
content.summary:
Vigilantes also took part in the fight that raged all night and the following morning, residents say

Nigerian soldiers killed more than 300 members of kidnapping and cattle bandit gangs in the north-western state of Zamfara this week, according to a government official.

Government troops targeted the gangs in Gummi district in a two-day operation that “led to the elimination of more than 300 terrorists”, Zamfara’s information commissioner, Mahmud Muhammad Dantawasa, said in a stat

content.title: Developing countries spend more repaying foreign debt than on education, UN reveals
content.summary:
Unesco report shows children lost out to servicing debt in 113 countries, with 18 spending five times more on loans

Most developing countries spent less on education than they did repaying debt last year, according to the UN, at the same time as global aid to education is predicted to decline by up to 30%.

More was spent on servicing foreign debt than on education in 113 developing countries in 2025, according to research by the UN’s culture and education agency, Unesco. In sub-Sa

content.title: Derision at US chain Chipotle’s plan to sell its Mexican food in Mexico
content.summary:
One social media user described the opening of its first outlet in Monterrey as ‘like the dog teaching a duck to fly’

A US chain’s plan to sell its version of Mexican food to Mexicans with a first branch south of the border has prompted bemusement, skepticism and anger among local people.

Chipotle Mexican Grill, known for its customisable burritos, tacos and bowls, has more than 4,000 locations worldwide and announced on Tuesday that it was expanding into Mexico in what it describ

content.title: ‘God is punishing the politicians’: anger at earthquake response grows in Venezuela
content.summary:
Threat of social unrest rises as public indignation at lack of disaster aid comes on top of fallout from US military intervention

• A revolution in ruins: fury amid the rubble of a housing project in quake-hit Venezuela

Public anger at what many perceive as

content.title: Mexico to demand criminal complaints over migrants killed by ICE in US
content.summary:
Claudia Sheinbaum says Mexicans ‘outraged’ over killing last week of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo by agents in Houston

Claudia Sheinbaum announced on Monday that Mexico would be demanding criminal complaints in the US for the deaths of more than a dozen Mexican migrants in immigration detention and those killed in anti-migrant operations.

The deaths include last week’

content.title: Experts warn of ways screwworm could spread in the US and new difficulties in keeping it at bay
content.summary:
Scientists worry that current eradication efforts won’t be able to contain parasitic infestation pushing into US

When conservationists set up cameras in remote regions of Central American forests, they wanted to monitor illegal cattle movement, which can lead to deforestation. But in recent months, they discovered another alarming development: wildlife rapidly infected with the new world screwworm.

It’s a warning

content.title: A revolution in ruins: fury amid the rubble of a housing project in quake-hit Venezuela
content.summary:
Discontent with Trump-backed government mounts as Chávez heirs struggle to respond to disaster for which they seem ill-prepared

Even before two powerful earthquakes reduced the OPPE 25 government housing project to an anarchy of shattered concrete and broken lives, the foundations of Hugo Chávez’s populist “Bolivarian” revolution were shaking in what was once a hotbed of support.

Gabriel González remembers his elation when, in 2013, he received the keys to his freshly completed ap

content.title: Ebola is spreading faster in eastern Congo than it can be tracked, as deaths pass 700
content.summary: Eighty percent of new Ebola cases in eastern Congo are emerging from unknown chains of transmission, according to WHO, a sign the outbreak is spreading faster than health officials can track.

content.title: Rep. Ro Khanna on being detained by Israeli settlers in the West Bank
content.summary: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with Rep. Ro Khanna, D-Calif., about his recent trip to the occupied West Bank and temporary detention by Israeli settlers and soldiers.

content.title: U.S. to reinstate Hormuz blockade. And, states sue over Paramount-Warner deal
content.summary: The United States plans to reinstate a blockade over the Strait of Hormuz today. And, several states are suing to stop the massive Paramount-Warner Bros. merger.

content.title: The U.S. is back to blockading Iran as the Strait of Hormuz standoff escalates
content.summary: The U.S. military says it's begun its blockade of ships entering or exiting Iran's ports, as Iran vows to assert its own control over the critical Strait of Hormuz.

content.title: Death toll from a Bangkok music bar fire rises to 30, dozens remain in hospital
content.summary: More than 70 people were injured, with 24 of them still in critical condition, according to a statement by the Bangkok Metropolitan Administration.

content.title: Pakistan urges restraint as its Mideast deal unravels
content.summary: Pakistan urges restraint as Iran and the U.S. clash and a memorandum of understanding unravels.

content.title: People watching the World Cup during work hours is leading to a drop in productivity
content.summary: The global economy may be suffering from lost productivity as people get swept by World Cup fever, a survey shows. This small business owner would know. She's spent most of her days watching matches.

content.title: The U.S. strikes Iran after Trump announces a renewed blockade and tolls in Hormuz
content.summary: The U.S. and Iran are fighting for control of the Strait of Hormuz, threatening a return to all-out war after agreeing to a ceasefire last month.

content.title: The U.S.-Iran ceasefire grows more distant. And, Congress faces a consequential week
content.summary: The U.S. and Iran exchanged fire over the weekend amid tensions over the Strait of Hormuz. And, Congress returns from recess facing tight deadlines.

content.title: Sam Neill, known for 'Jurassic Park' and 'The Piano,' dies at 78, his family says
content.summary: In 2023, Neill disclosed he had been diagnosed with angioimmunoblastic T-cell lymphoma, a rare type of non-Hodgkin lymphoma. Neill died on Monday in Sydney, according to a statement posted to the actor's social media page.

content.title: Trump threatens to bomb bridges and power plants unless Iran resumes talks
content.summary: Trump's comments aired as the two countries exchanged fire for the fourth day in a row and the US resumed blockading Iranian ports.

content.title: Trump retreat over Hormuz tolls suggests he is struggling to end Iran war
content.summary: The abrupt about-face from the US president was the latest twist in a conflict that has now lasted more than four months.

content.title: Strait of Hormuz 'faultline' exposes weakness of the US-Iran deal
content.summary: Control of the waterway has become a key point of contention between the US and Iran, as a ceasefire deal falters.

content.title: China economic growth falls sharply, missing target
content.summary: Weak demand domestically and the impact of the Iran war on oil prices overshadowed the country's strong exports.

content.title: Seven Britons among those killed in Spain wildfires
content.summary: Twelve of the 13 victims were foreign nationals, Spanish authorities said on Tuesday.

content.title: China detains US scientist who studied North Korea nuclear tests
content.summary: Chen Youlin's family says he has been wrongfully detained for nearly two years on spying charges.

content.title: Israeli strike on police post in north Gaza kills seven, officials say
content.summary: A senior officer in the Hamas-run police force was among those killed in the strike, which Israel's military says targeted "terrorists".

content.title: Trump pays writer E Jean Carroll $5m in damages over sexual abuse and defamation
content.summary: The president had sought to delay the payment as he tried to persuade the Supreme Court to overturn the judgement.

content.title: Diamond giant De Beers halts work at flagship South African mine as demand plummets
content.summary: Production will stop for two years at the mine which employs more than 4,000 people.

content.title: Tyrannosaurus rex most expensive dinosaur ever sold
content.summary: The dinosaur skeleton, which roamed the planet 67 million years ago, was sold by Sotheby's in New York.

content.title: Waiting for Hassan: Another Gaza doctor held by Israel without charge
content.summary: Hassan Khalil Almukayed is among at least 15 Palestinian doctors from Gaza currently in Israeli detention.

content.title: How the failed 2016 coup reshaped Turkiye’s civil-military relations
content.summary: Takeover bid 10 years ago sped up efforts to expand civilian oversight, experts say, reducing chances of another coup.

content.title: England vs Argentina: FIFA World Cup semifinal – Messi, Kane and prediction
content.summary: England and Argentina renew their World Cup rivalry after 24 years, as Harry Kane and Lionel Messi clash in the semis.

content.title: World Cup: England have ‘not peaked yet’, says Tuchel before semifinal
content.summary: England face Argentina in Wednesday's semifinal, hoping to reach the World Cup final for the first time since 1966.

content.title: How Iran’s southern coast is bearing the brunt of US attacks
content.summary: Donald Trump says the US is going to bomb Iran’s civilian infrastructure if there is no deal by next week.

content.title: Spain knock out France, prepare for World Cup final
content.summary: Spain beat France 2-0 to reach the World Cup final, shutting down Kylian Mbappe

content.title: T.Rex skeleton sells for $50.1 million at New York auction
content.summary: A T.Rex skeleton nicknamed ‘Gus’ sold for a record $50 million at an auction in New York.

content.title: Saving shea: How a Ugandan woman is turning waste into clean energy
content.summary: As demand for charcoal threatens indigenous trees, communities are finding new value in what was once treated as waste.

content.title: Nearly 75% of Americans think there’s too much money in politics
content.summary: The US is set to have some of the most expensive elections in history.

content.title: Trump welcomes Iraqi PM to White House, vows ‘a lot of deals’
content.summary: US President Donald Trump and Iraqi PM Ali al-Zaidi have met at the White House pledging to deepen economic ties.

content.title: Iran: Hormuz resterà chiuso fino a che non cesseranno le aggressioni Usa
content.summary: 'Colpite strutture militari americane in Bahrein'

content.title: Panama, il governo studia il futuro della miniera da 10 miliardi
content.summary: Nei prossimi mesi raccomandazione al presidente Mulino sul giacimento chiuso nel 2023

content.title: Colombia, il presidente eletto De la Espriella attacca l'accordo di pace con le Farc
content.summary: L'ex leader della guerriglia Timochenko avverte sui rischi di violenza e difende l'intesa

content.title: Parla il padre del colombiano ucciso dall'Ice, 'chiedo giustizia negli Usa'
content.summary: 'La sua morte è ingiusta, aveva il permesso di lavoro e documenti regolari'

content.title: Messico, si dimette il procuratore speciale federale Ulises Lara
content.summary: Lascia l'incarico per motivi personali dopo sei mesi

content.title: Venezuela, 15 ong chiedono agli Usa trasparenza sugli aiuti dopo il sisma
content.summary: Lettera a Rubio per garantire controlli indipendenti e rimuovere ostacoli ai soccorsi

content.title: Venezuela, Machado-González convocano opposizione dopo apertura del governo al dialogo
content.summary: Vertice domani per valutare la road map proposta tra esecutivo e il Parlamento del 2015

content.title: La Procura della Bolivia indaga sul reclutamento di giovani per la guerra in Ucraina
content.summary: Inchiesta aperta dopo il caso di due cugini partiti per la Russia e dati per morti

content.title: Trump, 'attacchi all'Iran continueranno fincheè non dirò basta'
content.summary: 'La settimana prossima colpiremo le centrali elettriche e i ponti'

content.title: Ong ricorre alla giustizia cubana per ritrovare il dissidente Otero Alcántara
content.summary: Cubalex chiede di chiarire dove si trovi l'artista dopo il trasferimento dal carcere

content.title: Deadly Russian strike hits Odesa as von der Leyen arrives in Ukraine for defence talks
content.summary: Russia carried out a strike on the port city of Odesa in Ukraine that killed three, a local official reported on Wednesday, as European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen arrived in Kyiv to announce new defence initiatives. Overnight, Ukraine also said it struck 20 Russian vessels in the Black Sea, including gas tankers.

content.title: Spain shuts down France and advances to the World Cup final with a 2-0 victory
content.summary: Spain’s defensive prowess and swagger were just too much for an attacking trio led by France great Kylian Mbappé, and just enough to get the 2010 champions into another World Cup final. Spain will play on Sunday against the winner between reigning champion Argentina and England on ⁠Wednesday. France will play in the third-place match on Saturday. FRANCE 24's Selina Sykes reports from the Dallas stadium where France and Spain played.

content.title: World Cup: French dream comes to an end against dominating Spain
content.summary: The French team were defeated by Spain (2–0) in the World Cup semi-final. France’s dream of reaching a third consecutive final – a feat achieved by only two other nations before them – has come to an end.

content.title: China's economic growth slows to weakest pace in over three years
content.summary: Economic reports on Wednesday found that Chinese growth has sunk to the slowest pace in over three years. Despite benefitting from the global AI boom, the country has not managed to offset the slump due to a struggling property sector, drop in domestic spending and trade disruptions caused by the war in Iran.

content.title: Middle East live: More than 30 killed in recent US strikes, Iranian government says
content.summary: An Iranian government spokesperson on Wednesday said more than 30 civilians were killed in US air strikes targeting the country’s southern region in “recent days”. Tehran was quick to retaliate with Jordan, Kuwait and Bahrain all reporting attacks. The strike exchanges come as the US naval blockade on Iranian ports resumed. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

content.title: World Cup 2026: Which team are supporters from eliminated nations rooting for?
content.summary: During the France-Spain semi-final, many supporters at the FIFA fan zone in Dallas were wearing jerseys from nations that got eliminated earlier in the competition. We went to ask them which team they were supporting now.

content.title: Publishers sue Google over alleged use of books to train AI models
content.summary: Book publishers sued Google on Tuesday, accusing the tech giant of illegally using copyrighted works to train its artificial intelligence models and generate content that competes with human authors. The lawsuit marks the latest legal battle over how AI developers use books and other creative works to build their systems.

content.title: “We’ve given the French a proper trashing": the Spaniards delighted, the French disappointed
content.summary: “We’ve given the French a proper trashing.” In Dallas, Spanish fans were delighted, whilst French fans were disappointed, following Spain’s 2-0 victory over France in the World Cup semi-final.

content.title: World Cup 2026: In Dallas, Spanish supporters celebrate their team's victory over France
content.summary: Spain have just won their World Cup semi-final against France. In the FIFA fan zone in Dallas, Spanish supporters celebrated this 2-0 victory.

content.title: France's World Cup dream extinguished in chastening semi-final loss to Spain
content.summary: Didier Deschamps' France were hot favourites to lift the famous golden trophy after a string of breathtaking attacking displays at the World Cup. But they met their match in the semi-finals when faced with slick European champions Spain, who outplayed and outsmarted Les Bleus to deny them a third consecutive final.

content.title: Volodymyr Zelenskyy pushes for new government in Ukraine
content.summary: Ukrainian Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko stepped down after barely a year in office. President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is already in talks with potential successors. What can Ukraine expect from the Cabinet reshuffle?

content.title: How the 2016 coup attempt changed Turkey for good
content.summary: Ten years ago, parts of the Turkish military attempted to overthrow the government of President Recep Tayyip Erdogan. The consequences of the failed coup continue to shape Turkey to this day. DW looks back.

content.title: US troop withdrawal from Iraq set for September 30
content.summary: The planned US withdrawal is linked to the disarmament of influential Iran-backed militias in Iraq.

content.title: US-Iraq relations: Can Iran-allied militias be disarmed?
content.summary: When Donald Trump meets Iraq's new PM today, they are likely to discuss disarming Iran's allies in Iraq. Some have already pledged to give up their guns. But is that likely? And will it influence the US-Iran war?

content.title: Ukraine parliament accepts PM Svyrydenko's resignation
content.summary: It comes as President Volodymyr Zelenskyy moves to reshuffle the cabinet as Ukraine was "changing its political strategy."

content.title: Nigeria offers clues to new US Africa strategy
content.summary: The withdrawal of US troops from Nigeria after the killing of an "Islamic State" leader may be less about leaving Africa than redefining how Washington fights terrorism on the continent.

content.title: France arrests arson suspects amid Fontainebleau forest fire
content.summary: Firefighters are attempting to tackle a major fire burning since Sunday in the much-visited Fontainebleau forest, near France's capital Paris. Two people have been arrested for suspected arson.

content.title: Person killed in ICE-involved shooting in Maine
content.summary: US Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents were involved in a fatal shooting in Biddeford, according to Maine's House speaker. It comes days after ICE allegedly shot a man in Texas during a traffic stop.

content.title: South Korea airs concerns over North Korean border activity
content.summary: South Korea has voiced concern that its neighbor's actions along the demilitarized zone between the two countries may be in breach of the 1953 armistice agreement.

content.title: Iran: Living in limbo between war and ceasefire
content.summary: Repeated ceasefire violations, contradictory political signals and ongoing emergency measures have fueled exhaustion, anger and deep uncertainty among many Iranians.

content.title: Lunar Gateway: Earth’s guard post against asteroids?
content.summary: Humankind’s next space outpost, the lunar Gateway, will serve as a staging point to reach the surface of the Moon. A new ESA-backed study is considering whether it could also be used as a deployment point for planetary defence missions, to intercept asteroids approaching dangerously close to Earth.

content.title: ESA commissions world’s first space debris removal
content.summary: ClearSpace-1 will be the first space mission to remove an item of debris from orbit, planned for launch in 2025. The mission is being procured as a service contract with a startup-led commercial consortium, to help establish a new market for in-orbit servicing, as well as debris removal.

content.title: Hera’s radar CubeSat will peer into asteroid’s heart
content.summary: ESA’s proposed Hera asteroid mission for planetary defence will carry the smallest radar instrument ever flown in space – flying on a separate mini-spacecraft, it will approach two asteroids closer than its mothership and probe deep into their cores.

content.title: Microsoft Patches a Record 570 Security Flaws
content.summary: Microsoft Corp. today released software updates to plug at least 570 security holes in its Windows operating systems and other software, almost triple the number of vulnerabilities the software giant fixed in its record-smashing Patch Tuesday release last month. Microsoft attributed the burgeoning patch counts to vulnerability discoveries aided by artificial intelligence.

content.title: Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak
content.summary: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued a postmortem on a data leak in which a contractor published dozens of internal CISA credentials -- including AWS Govcloud keys -- in a public GitHub repository for almost six months before being notified by KrebsOnSecurity. Experts say the gaps identified in the agency's initial response provide important lessons that all security teams should absorb.

content.title: Felons, Fraudsters Flog Offensive Cybersecurity Startup
content.summary: A cybersecurity startup dangling millions of dollars to acquire zero-day security vulnerabilities in popular software is run by a pair of far-right conspiracy theorists and convicted felons whose most recent ventures included fake intelligence companies and a now-defunct AI-based lobbying platform they operated under assumed names.

content.title: FBI Seizes NetNut Proxy Platform, Popa Botnet
content.summary: The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) said today it worked with industry partners to seize hundreds of domains associated with NetNut, a sprawling residential proxy service operated by the publicly-traded Israeli company Alarum Technologies [NASDAQ: ALAR]. The action comes roughly two weeks after KrebsOnSecurity published findings from multiple security firms connecting NetNut to the Popa botnet, a collection of at least two million devices that have been compromised by malicious software wi

content.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
content.summary: Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport network in the Greater London area. The duo were key members of a prolific cybercrime group known as Scattered Spider, and their guilty pleas came on the first day of what was expected to be a six-week trial.

content.title: ‘Popa’ Botnet Linked to Publicly-Traded Israeli Firm
content.summary: For the past four years, a sprawling Android-based botnet called Popa has forced millions of consumer TV boxes to relay Internet traffic linked to advertising fraud, account takeovers, and mass data-scraping efforts. This week, researchers from multiple security firms concluded that the Popa botnet is linked to NetNut, a "residential proxy" provider operated by the publicly-traded Israeli firm Alarum Technologies Ltd [NASDAQ: ALAR].

content.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
content.summary: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.

content.title: A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
content.summary: Microsoft today released software updates to plug nearly 200 security holes across its Windows operating systems and supported software, a record number of fixes for the company's monthly Patch Tuesday cycle. Nearly three dozen of those bugs earned Microsoft's most dire "critical" rating, and exploit code for at least three of the weaknesses is now publicly available.

content.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
content.summary: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.

content.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
content.summary: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequ

content.title: Global liquefied natural gas trade volumes reached record high in 2025
content.summary: Global liquefied natural gas (LNG) trade volumes increased 5.4% to a record 56.3 billion cubic feet per day last year (Bcf/d), driven largely by U.S. LNG export capacity expanding to meet growing demand, according to a recent report from the International Group of Liquefied Natural Gas Importers (GIIGNL). Global LNG trade has slowed this year following the closure of the key export route for Qatar, the world's second-largest LNG exporter.

content.title: The United States produced more crude oil than any other country in 2025
content.summary: The United States remained the world's largest crude oil producer in 2025, according to our International Energy Statistics database, extending a streak that began in 2018 when the United States overtook Russia to become the world's leading producer.

content.title: U.S. exports of crude oil and petroleum products reached record in April
content.summary: U.S. petroleum exports reached a record in April as disruptions to international crude oil and refined product flows through the Strait of Hormuz increased global demand for U.S. exports. Exports increased to 13.6 million barrels per day (b/d) in April, 15% more than the previous record set in March.

content.title: The 250-year history of U.S. energy consumption
content.summary: Over the 250-year history of our nation, energy consumption has evolved from wood use in the 18th and 19th centuries to today's use of modern renewable, hydrocarbon, and nuclear technology. In 2025, total energy used in the United States was 96 quadrillion British thermal units (quads), up 2% from 2024, but below 2007's record 99 quads. Petroleum was the most-used energy source last year, followed closely by natural gas. Use of renewable, coal, and nuclear energy each made up about 9% of total e

content.title: U.S. refining capacity decreased during 2025
content.summary: U.S. operable atmospheric distillation capacity, the primary measure of refinery capacity, totaled 18.2 million barrels per calendar day (b/cd) on January 1, 2026-down over 250,000 b/cd (about 1%) compared with January 1, 2025-according to our latest annual Refinery Capacity Report.

content.title: Metered electricity demand in the New York ISO falls midday because of small-scale solar
content.summary: An increase in electricity generation from small-scale solar in New York has decreased the midday demand for metered electricity, amid overall declining load in the state. The trend is particularly notable in the early spring (March and April), when solar generation has an outsized impact because demand is relatively low and conditions for solar generation are favorable.

content.title: U.S. commercial crude oil inventories have decreased in June
content.summary: For the week ending June 19, 2026, U.S. refineries processed 17.1 million barrels per day (b/d) of crude oil, down 81,000 b/d from the previous week, and they operated at 96.1% capacity utilization. Gasoline production averaged 9.5 million b/d, and distillate production increased to 5.2 million b/d.

content.title: UAE's exit from OPEC+ reduced the group's share of crude oil production and capacity
content.summary: On April 28, 2026, the United Arab Emirates (UAE) announced that it was leaving OPEC, effective on May 1. OPEC was formed in 1960 by Iraq, Iran, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and Venezuela, with the stated objective to "coordinate and unify petroleum policies among Member Countries." OPEC is best known for its effect on global crude oil prices.

content.title: Permian natural gas production increased faster than crude oil
content.summary: The Permian region's marketed natural gas production grew from 17.2 billion cubic feet per day (Bcf/d) in 2021 to 27.6 Bcf/d in 2025, a 60% increase, according to data from our latest Short Term Energy Outlook. Over the same period, crude oil production grew by 39%, going from 4.7 million barrels per day (b/d) to 6.6 million b/d. The higher growth in natural gas production is the result of increasing gas-oil ratios (GOR).

content.title: Solar generation in CAISO surpassed natural gas in the first five months of 2026
content.summary: In the first five months of 2026, utility-scale solar generation surpassed natural gas generation in CAISO. Solar electricity generation in the California Independent System Operator (CAISO) over the first five months of 2026 increased 21% compared with the same period in 2024, and natural gas generation decreased by 60%, data from our Hourly Electric Grid Monitor shows.

content.title: Arera disegna il settore energetico fino al 2029
content.summary:
L’Autorità di Regolazione per Energia, Reti e Ambiente ha posto in consultazione fino al 15 settembre il Quadro strategico 2026-2029. Il documento programmatorio (disponibile in basso) interviene su molti argomenti della più stringente attualità, dalla riforma del mercato elettrico ai prezzi zonali, passando per le connessioni di impianti rinnovabili, la gestione attiva della domanda, i […]

The post

content.title: A giugno il fotovoltaico ha prodotto un quarto dell’elettricità Ue
content.summary:
A giugno 2026, per la prima volta in assoluto, il fotovoltaico ha prodotto un quarto dell’elettricità complessiva nell’Ue: 52 TWh, pari al 25% della produzione mensile dei 27 Stati membri. Il dato supera il precedente record mensile di 47 TWh (23% del mix elettrico) registrato il mese precedente, maggio 2026. Secondo dati raccolti e diffusi […]

The post A giugno il fotovo

content.title: Appalti pubblici: decarbonizzazione e filiere europee nella nuova bozza Ue
content.summary:
Qualità di norma con un peso minimo del 30%, emissioni e altri costi ambientali conteggiabili lungo il ciclo di vita, requisiti di efficienza energetica e possibili preferenze per prodotti europei e da filiere sicure. Sono alcune delle novità della bozza del regolamento Ue sugli appalti pubblici che sta circolando in questi giorni (pdf in basso). […]

content.title: Il Tar sui termini procedimentali della Pas
content.summary:
Una nuova sentenza della Giustizia amministrativa tenta di rendere più chiari e fluidi i passaggi dei procedimenti autorizzativi per gli impianti rinnovabili. Il Tar Brescia è infatti intervenuto su un caso riguardante la procedura abilitativa semplificata (Pas) per la riconversione di un impianto da biogas a biometano, stabilendo quando si applicano le disposizioni del D.Lgs. content.title: La Cina può produrre più batterie della domanda mondiale
content.summary:
La Cina potrebbe arrivare al 2030 con una capacità produttiva di celle per batterie superiore all’intera domanda mondiale. Secondo il Carnegie Endowment for International Peace, gli impianti cinesi già in costruzione porterebbero la capacità ad almeno 5.862 GWh l’anno, con un potenziale massimo di 6.720 GWh, contro una domanda globale stimata fra 3.600 e 5.100 […]

The post La Cina

content.title: WeCo inaugura la produzione europea di batterie
content.summary:
WeCo annuncia l’avvio della nuova produzione europea di sistemi di accumulo. Le nuove batterie saranno realizzate con Battery Management System (BMS) di origine europea e identificati dal marchio Euro 1, a testimonianza di un processo produttivo interamente sviluppato in Europa, dalla progettazione elettronica all’assemblaggio finale. Cuore della nuova linea produttiva è anche il nuovo inverter […]

content.title: Oil Hits One-Month High
content.summary: Oil climbed to a one-month high as renewed Middle East fighting heightened concerns over supplies through the Strait of Hormuz.

content.title: Vedanta Oil to Invest $5B to Boost Production Fivefold
content.summary: Vedanta Oil and Gas Ltd. will invest $5 billion to expand production by more than five times.

content.title: Ukraine Hits Russian Refineries, Ships, Moscow Hits Odesa
content.summary: Kyiv struck a slew of Russian energy targets overnight, while Moscow hit fuel facilities in the Ukrainian port of Odesa.

content.title: North America Drops Rigs for 1st Time in Months
content.summary: North America's rig count dropped by 10 rigs week on week, according to Baker Hughes' latest North America rotary rig count.

content.title: Escalations Damage Hormuz Media Narrative
content.summary: Escalations over the past week have damaged the media narrative that has built up over the past few weeks, Standard Chartered Bank Energy Research Head Emily Ashford told Rigzone.

content.title: Cross-Industry Group Issues Letter to Incoming UK Gov
content.summary: A 'powerful' cross-industry group is delivering a 'strongly worded letter' to the incoming UK government, a statement sent to Rigzone by OEUK recently noted.

content.title: BP Flags $1B Writedown from Low Carbon Business
content.summary: BP said it expects to write down another $1 billion from energy transition assets in the second quarter.

content.title: Houthis Strike Saudi Arabia in Major Escalation
content.summary: The Iran-backed Houthi group in Yemen fired ballistic missiles and drones on Saudi Arabia.

content.title: Oil Soars on Hormuz Blockade
content.summary: Oil surged after President Trump announced plans to reinstate a blockade on Iranian shipping and impose fees on cargo moving through the Strait of Hormuz.

content.title: Russian Refinery Runs Plunge to Lowest in More Than 2 Decades
content.summary: A wave of Ukrainian attacks has pushed Russian refining runs to the lowest in more than 21 years.
